data.title: Iran Considers Strait of Hormuz Blockade as Retaliatory Measure
data.description: Iran's Supreme Leader's office has reiterated the possibility of using a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz as a retaliatory measure. According to NS3.AI, details from Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei’s first public speech, shared on social media on April 1, indicate that Iran is also evaluating other potential fronts. The office stated that these fronts would be activated only after a thorough assessment of relevant factors if the conflict persists.

data.title: EdgeX Initiates Buyback Following Token Lockup
data.description: EdgeX has commenced a buyback of its EDGE tokens after previously locking up 140 million tokens, which represents 14% of the total supply. According to NS3.AI, the exchange announced that the earlier lockup is set to last for one year.

data.title: SEC Approves Rule Change for Multi-Crypto Asset Trust Options on NYSE American
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has announced the approval of a rule change application for listing multi-cryptocurrency asset trust options on NYSE American. According to BlockBeats, this marks an expansion from previously allowing options for single cryptocurrency asset trusts to now supporting options for trusts holding multiple crypto assets.

The prerequisites for listing include each cryptocurrency asset held by the trust meeting high liquidity standards, with an average daily market value of at least $700 million over the past 12 months. Additionally, derivative contracts for these assets must have comprehensive surveillance sharing agreements with trading platforms.

Trust shares must adhere to the initial and ongoing listing standards for ETF options on trading platforms and be classified as NMS stocks.

The SEC's review concluded that the rule change will provide investors with greater exposure to cryptocurrency assets and hedging tools without requiring separate SEC approval for each instance, thereby enhancing market efficiency.

data.title: Yesterday, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 1,752 BTC, and the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 14,488 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, yesterday the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 1,752 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 14,488 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 571 SOL.

data.title: U.S. Stock Indices Continue to Rise as S&P 500 Reaches 6600 Points
data.description: The major U.S. stock indices have seen their gains continue to expand, with the S&P 500 index reaching 6600 points. According to Jin10, the Dow Jones Industrial Average is currently up by approximately 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite Index has increased by about 1.7%. This upward trend reflects a positive sentiment in the market as investors react to recent developments.

data.title: Google's Quantum AI Team Suggests Fewer Qubits Needed to Break Bitcoin Cryptography
data.description: Google's Quantum AI team has indicated that breaking the cryptography used by Bitcoin and Ethereum might require fewer than 500,000 physical qubits, challenging the commonly cited figure of millions. According to NS3.AI, this revelation could have significant implications for the security of blockchain technologies. In a separate development, Australia has enacted a digital asset law mandating that cryptocurrency exchanges and custody providers secure financial services licenses. This legislative move aims to enhance regulatory oversight and consumer protection in the rapidly evolving digital asset sector.

data.title: Oil Traders Face Losses Amid War-Driven Price Fluctuations
data.description:
A group of oil traders has heavily shorted the market, betting on a decline in oil prices from their war-driven highs, but most have faced significant losses. According to Odaily, data shows that in March, ETF investors poured $977 million into the ProShares UltraShort Bloomberg Crude Oil ETF (SCO), marking the largest monthly inflow since the fund's inception in 2008. SCO offers twice the inverse daily return of oil price changes. Despite the record inflow, SCO's total assets remain at $970 million, below the total monthly inflow.

Rocky Fishman, founder of Asym 500, stated, "This is a bet on 'the war ending soon.'" Following U.S. President Donald Trump's suggestion of a potential end to the Iran war, the fund rose by 8%, yet it still fell by 41% in March, marking its worst performance in nearly six years. However, short positions represent only half of the market picture, as long funds also set records. The United States Oil Fund (USO) attracted approximately $700 million in March, the largest monthly inflow since the pandemic began, while the United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) drew $600 million, reaching an all-time high. The market remains highly divided, with leveraged funds hedging bets on both sides.

data.title: Asian ETFs Experience Record Outflows Before April Rebound
data.description: Exchange-traded funds (ETFs) listed in the United States that track single Asian countries, including India and Taiwan, experienced unprecedented outflows in March. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant withdrawal of funds just before a notable recovery in the region's equities on April 1. This trend reflects investor sentiment and market dynamics affecting Asian financial markets.

data.title: Expert Criticizes Token Voting in Crypto Governance
data.description: Francesco Mosterts has raised concerns about the effectiveness of token voting as a governance system in the cryptocurrency sector. According to NS3.AI, Mosterts referenced a study of 50 decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs), highlighting that a single large voter could influence 35% of the outcomes. He pointed out issues such as low participation, dominance by large stakeholders, and insufficient incentives, which result in governance being led by passive opinions rather than informed decisions. As a solution, Mosterts suggested the adoption of decision markets as an alternative approach.

data.title: Human Rights Foundation Allocates 1.5 Billion Satoshis to Bitcoin Projects
data.description: The Human Rights Foundation (HRF) has distributed 1.5 billion satoshis through its Bitcoin Development Fund to support 26 projects aimed at enhancing Bitcoin infrastructure, privacy, payments, and education. According to NS3.AI, these grants are intended for open-source developers, researchers, and community initiatives across regions including Asia, Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean. The initiative underscores HRF's commitment to fostering innovation and development within the Bitcoin ecosystem.

data.title: Iran reiterated its intention to blockade the Strait of Hormuz as a retaliatory measure.
data.description: On April 1, the office of Iran's Supreme Leader released excerpts of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's first speech on social media, reiterating the continued use of the strait blockade as a countermeasure. The office also released excerpts stating that Iran is exploring opening "other potential fronts," believing its adversaries "lack experience and have obvious weaknesses" in these areas. If the conflict continues, these fronts will be activated after comprehensively considering relevant factors. (CCTV News)

data.title: Empery Digital transferred 1795 BTC to Gemini one hour ago.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital transferred 1,795 BTC, worth $122.53 million, to cryptocurrency exchange Gemini one hour ago.

data.title: Eli Lilly's Stock Rises Over 4% Following U.S. Approval of Weight Loss Drug Foundayo
data.description: Eli Lilly's shares saw a significant increase of over 4% after the company's weight loss drug, Foundayo, received approval in the United States. According to Jin10, this development marks a pivotal moment for Eli Lilly as it expands its portfolio in the lucrative weight loss market. The approval is expected to boost the company's revenue and strengthen its position in the pharmaceutical industry. Investors are optimistic about the potential impact of Foundayo on Eli Lilly's future growth, reflecting positively in the stock market.

data.title: SpaceX is expected to go public in June, with a valuation potentially exceeding $1.75 trillion.
data.description: Sources familiar with the matter revealed that SpaceX has secretly filed for an IPO, bringing it one step closer to becoming the largest IPO in history. The sources said SpaceX has submitted a draft IPO registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and is expected to list in June, becoming the first of three potential mega-IPOs, ahead of OpenAI and Anthropic. The sources indicated that SpaceX may seek a valuation exceeding $1.75 trillion in its IPO. The company previously acquired Musk's artificial intelligence startup xAI, bringing the combined entity's valuation to $1.25 trillion. Details of the listing, including the number of shares to be sold and the price range, are expected to be disclosed in subsequent filings. SpaceX's IPO could raise as much as $75 billion, far exceeding the current record holder Saudi Aramco's $29 billion in 2019. The sources said that banks including Bank of America, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan Chase, and Morgan Stanley will serve as the lead underwriters for the IPO. (Jinshi)

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Space Stocks Surge with Intuitive Machines Leading Gains
data.description: U.S. space stocks experienced a notable rise, with Intuitive Machines leading the gains by climbing 8.7%. According to Jin10, Rocket Lab followed with a 4.8% increase, while Planet Labs saw an 8.4% uptick. Howmet Aerospace also contributed to the upward trend, rising by 3.1%. This surge reflects growing investor interest in the aerospace sector, driven by advancements and strategic developments within these companies.

data.title: Far East Consortium Sells Stake in Melbourne Ritz-Carlton for Capital Optimization
data.description:
Far East Consortium International Limited has announced the sale of a 49.9% stake in the company owning the Ritz-Carlton Hotel in Melbourne, Australia, for nearly HKD 315 million. According to Ming Pao, the stake, along with associated debts, was sold to a Singapore-based holding company owned by the group's chairman, David Chiu, and his daughter, executive director Winnie Chiu.

The group stated that this transaction will help realize the value of the hotel, allowing the proceeds to be utilized for capital reinvestment. Additionally, it aims to increase liquidity and reduce the group's net asset-liability ratio.

data.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Reportedly Files for Secret IPO
data.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX has reportedly filed a confidential application for an initial public offering (IPO), according to market news. According to NS3.AI, the information was initially cited by Jin10. Details about the IPO, including the timeline and valuation, remain undisclosed. SpaceX, known for its ambitious space exploration and satellite projects, has been a significant player in the aerospace industry. The potential IPO could mark a pivotal moment for the company, attracting substantial investor interest.

data.title: Forecast Market Volume Expected to Reach $75 Billion in Q1 2026
data.description: The forecast market is projected to reach a record trading volume of $75 billion in the first quarter of 2026, according to CryptoRank data. According to ChainCatcher, this marks a significant increase from the $44 billion recorded in the fourth quarter of 2025, representing a growth of approximately 70%. Over the past two years, the forecast market has rapidly expanded, becoming a crucial part of the cryptocurrency sector.

data.title: New York gold futures first broke through the $4,800 mark
data.description: According to Gate data, New York gold futures touched $4,800 per ounce, up 2.60% on the day. Spot gold is currently up 2.25% at $4,773 per ounce.

data.title: SpaceX has secretly filed for an IPO.
data.description: According to market sources, Elon Musk's SpaceX has secretly filed for an IPO. (Jinshi)
